decisions.title: AI interpretation runs create background tasks with notifications.
decisions.detail: Each AI interpretation run generates a task that executes in the background within the task list, similar to the CC platform's operation. A notification will be sent upon its completion.

evidence.quote: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes

decisions.title: AI interpretation task completion notification details pending confirmation.
decisions.detail: The specific details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion require confirmation in the upcoming requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: AI interpretation uses two image sources.
decisions.detail: The AI interpretation process will utilize two distinct image sources: a large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained once from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded directly by the user.

evidence.quote: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user

decisions.title: User confirms suspected points after AI interpretation.
decisions.detail: Following the completion of AI interpretation, a list of suspected points is generated. The user will then have the option to confirm these points (yes/no).

evidence.quote: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

decisions.title: User decides to place suspected points under monitoring.
decisions.detail: After AI interpretation and confirmation of suspected points, the user can decide whether to place them under a watch or monitoring status.

evidence.quote: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

decisions.title: Reason for monitoring required when placing a point under watch.
decisions.detail: When a user decides to place a suspected point under monitoring, they must provide a reason.

evidence.quote: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded

decisions.title: Polygon upload required for monitoring points.
decisions.detail: When placing a suspected point under monitoring, a corresponding polygon must be uploaded.

evidence.quote: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded

decisions.title: Large-area polygons are valid for monitoring points.
decisions.detail: Polygons with large areas are considered valid for placing points under monitoring.

evidence.quote: (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

decisions.title: Overly small monitoring polygons are automatically filtered out.
decisions.detail: Polygons representing overly small areas, when uploaded for monitoring, will be automatically filtered out by the system.

evidence.quote: (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

decisions.title: Images must not be uploaded more than once due to file size.
decisions.detail: To manage large image file sizes, a constraint is imposed that images must not be uploaded more than a single time.

evidence.quote: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

decisions.title: Duplicate-prevention mechanism needed for image uploads.
decisions.detail: A mechanism is required to prevent duplicate image uploads, primarily due to the large size of image files.

evidence.quote: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

decisions.title: Two-period image comparison will not use the image library.
decisions.detail: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer retrieve images from the central image library.

evidence.quote: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

decisions.title: User manually annotates images for two-period comparison.
decisions.detail: For two-period image comparison, users will be responsible for manually annotating the images instead of pulling them from the image library.

evidence.quote: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

decisions.title: On-site workstation for image comparison.
decisions.detail: A dedicated workstation will be installed on-site to handle image comparison tasks.

evidence.quote: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

decisions.title: Image comparison model triggered by client's AP via workstation.
decisions.detail: Images for comparison will be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which will then initiate a call to the on-site workstation to run the comparison model.

evidence.quote: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

decisions.title: Base map provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
decisions.detail: The foundational map data will be supplied by GIS Provider B, leveraging GeoServer for delivery.

evidence.quote: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

decisions.title: Map viewer loads historically uploaded imagery.
decisions.detail: The map viewer component must be capable of loading and displaying imagery that has been uploaded historically.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map viewer filters imagery by date.
decisions.detail: The map viewer will support filtering the list of loaded imagery based on its upload or capture date.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map viewer filters imagery by image-name keyword.
decisions.detail: The map viewer will allow users to filter the list of imagery using keywords present in the image names.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map viewer filters imagery by type.
decisions.detail: The map viewer will enable filtering of imagery based on its type, including satellite, aerial photo, or drone footage.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map viewer displays points on the map.
decisions.detail: The map viewer is required to display various points of interest directly on the map interface.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Locate button displayed when opening a point.
decisions.detail: Upon opening a specific point on the map, a 'locate' button will be presented to the user.

evidence.quote: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

decisions.title: Map automatically zooms to point location on 'locate' click.
decisions.detail: When the 'locate' button associated with a point is clicked, the map view will automatically zoom to the precise location of that point.

evidence.quote: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

decisions.title: Imagery reverts to point display when zoomed out past a scale.
decisions.detail: If the map is zoomed out beyond a predetermined scale, the displayed imagery will automatically switch to a point-only representation.

evidence.quote: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

decisions.title: Map view locked in comparison mode.
decisions.detail: While in comparison mode, the map view will be locked, preventing any panning by the user.

evidence.quote: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

decisions.title: Release drag mode button restores panning in comparison mode.
decisions.detail: In comparison mode, a 'release drag mode' button will be available, allowing the user to restore the ability to pan the map.

evidence.quote: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

decisions.title: Users can adjust image transparency in map viewer.
decisions.detail: The map viewer will provide functionality for users to adjust the transparency level of images.

evidence.quote: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

decisions.title: Image transparency adjustment is important for orthophoto analysis.
decisions.detail: The ability to adjust image transparency is considered an important feature, specifically for the purpose of orthophoto analysis.

evidence.quote: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

decisions.title: Map viewer design references Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
decisions.detail: The design of the new map viewer should draw inspiration and reference from the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.

evidence.quote: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

decisions.title: Projects are organized by year.
decisions.detail: Projects within the system will be structured and organized on a yearly basis.

evidence.quote: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

decisions.title: Suspected points can be created within a project.
decisions.detail: The system will allow for the creation of suspected points as elements within an existing project.

evidence.quote: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

decisions.title: Confirmation needed on splitting suspected point layers by county/city.
decisions.detail: A decision is pending on whether the suspected point layers should be divided and organized by county/city; this requires confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon.
decisions.detail: For each suspected point, a corresponding polygon must be uploaded to the system.

evidence.quote: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

decisions.title: No image upload needed for suspected points.
decisions.detail: It is explicitly stated that there is no requirement for image uploads when creating or managing suspected points.

evidence.quote: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

decisions.title: Each year forms one layer for suspected points.
decisions.detail: The system will manage suspected points such that all points pertaining to a given year will collectively form a single layer.

evidence.quote: Each year forms one layer

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data interface to be added.
decisions.detail: A new interface for 'New Survey Point Data' must be incorporated into the system.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data interface must include specific fields.
decisions.detail: The 'New Survey Point Data' interface must include fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone'.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data interface refers to manual figure 2.13.
decisions.detail: The design and implementation of the 'New Survey Point Data' interface should reference and follow manual figure 2.13.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data form fields specified.
decisions.detail: The 'New Survey Point Data' form includes fields for survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.

evidence.quote: [Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]

decisions.title: Original MS file management system to be migrated.
decisions.detail: The existing MS file management system will be transitioned and integrated into the new system.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

decisions.title: Migrated MIS system interface to be redesigned.
decisions.detail: Although the functionality will remain the same, the user interface for the migrated MIS system will undergo a redesign.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

decisions.title: Migrated MIS system functionality to remain unchanged.
decisions.detail: Despite the migration and interface redesign, the core functionality of the legacy MIS system will be preserved and remain the same in the new system.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

decisions.title: MIS migration driven by information-security compliance.
decisions.detail: The primary motivation for migrating the legacy MIS system is to meet information-security compliance requirements.

evidence.quote: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements

decisions.title: Legacy MIS system migration details pending confirmation.

evidence.quote: The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification

evidence.quote: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

decisions.title: A-Bao requires data-flow document for AI interpretation.
decisions.detail: A-Bao has requested a data-flow document that explains the movement of data subsequent to AI interpretation, specifying data sources and computation locations.

evidence.quote: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens

decisions.detail: The requested data-flow document must detail where data is sourced from and where the computation processes occur after AI interpretation.

evidence.quote: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens

decisions.detail: It needs to be confirmed whether the layer containing suspected points should be geographically subdivided based on county/city boundaries.

implementation_scope: Implement AI interpretation background task processing, including task list integration and completion notifications (D001).
Develop image ingestion modules for two distinct sources: bulk Vendor A imagery and individual user uploads (D003).
Implement user interface and backend logic for confirming AI-generated suspected points (D004).
Implement workflow for users to mark suspected points for monitoring, including reason and polygon upload (D005, D006, D007).
Develop polygon validation logic: allow large areas, filter out overly small areas (D008, D009).
Implement a duplicate-prevention mechanism for image uploads (D011).
Modify two-period image comparison feature to support manual image annotation, removing reliance on central image library (D012, D013).
Develop Map Viewer component to load and display historical imagery (D017).
Implement Map Viewer functionality to filter imagery by date, image-name keyword, and type (satellite/aerial/drone) (D018, D019, D020).
Implement Map Viewer functionality to display points of interest (D021).
Implement 'Locate' button functionality in Map Viewer to zoom to selected points (D022, D023).
Implement automatic switch in Map Viewer to point display when zoomed out past a certain scale (D024).
Implement Map Viewer comparison mode with locked panning and a 'release drag mode' button (D025, D026).
Implement image transparency adjustment functionality in Map Viewer (D027).
Implement project structure organized by year (D030).
Develop functionality to create suspected points within projects (D031).
Implement suspected point data model requiring polygon upload, with no image upload (D033, D034).
Manage suspected points such that all points for a given year form a single layer (D035).

Develop 'New Survey Point Data' interface with specified fields (drawing number, key monitoring zone, survey serial number, village, monitoring date, method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y, lat/lon, approximate location) (D036, D037, D039).
Migrate original MS file management system, preserving functionality but redesigning the interface (D040, D041, D042).
Develop Follow-up module to integrate selected suspected points (D046).
Add a schedule-tracking interface to the Follow-up module (e.g., showing days remaining) (D047).
Implement the follow-up dispatch interface (D048).
Connect completed follow-up actions to survey-report management, including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D049, D050).
Create data-flow documentation for AI interpretation covering image source, workstation task execution, result output, and monitoring database (D051, D052, D053).

constraints.text: Image uploads must not be duplicated due to large file sizes, requiring a robust duplicate-prevention mechanism (D010, D011).
The image comparison model must run on an on-site workstation, triggered by the client's Access Point (D014, D015).
The base map data will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D016).
The Map Viewer's design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D029).
The 'New Survey Point Data' interface design must refer to manual figure 2.13 (D038, D057).
The migration of the legacy MIS system is driven by information-security compliance requirements (D043).
Project kick-off is expected in mid-May if bidding proceeds smoothly (D055).
An immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may be needed on the afternoon the bid is awarded (D056).
Sherry is responsible for completing the 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18 (D057).
Sherry is responsible for preparing the data-flow document for A-Bao's review by 2026-05-18 (D059).
First requirements interview scheduled for 2026-05-20 (D062, D063), requiring preparation of existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, and data flow diagram (D064).

architectural_decisions.text: AI interpretation tasks will be executed in the background within a task list, mirroring the operational model of the existing CC platform (D001).

open_technical_questions: What are the specific technical details and behavior of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., channels, payload structure, retry logic, error handling)? (D002, D065)
Should suspected point layers be split and organized by county/city? This impacts data modeling, querying, and potentially spatial indexing strategies. (D032, D066)
What are the specific interface design details (e.g., API contracts, UI component interactions) required for the MIS migration? (D044, D067)
Who will receive follow-up notifications and according to what schedule? This impacts the configuration and triggering logic of the notification service. (D068)

acceptance_criteria: Given an AI interpretation run is initiated, Then a task is created in the background task list with a 'running' status.
Given an AI interpretation task is running, When the task completes successfully, Then a completion notification is sent to the user.
Given an AI interpretation task is running, When the task fails, Then a failure notification is sent to the user.
Given the option to initiate AI interpretation, Then the user can select a nationwide batch of imagery from Vendor A.
Given the option to initiate AI interpretation, Then the user can upload individual images.
Given AI interpretation completes and generates suspected points, Then a list of these points is presented to the user.
Given a list of suspected points is presented, Then the user can confirm each point (Yes/No).
Given a user confirms a suspected point, Then the user is presented with the option to place it under watch/monitoring.
Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the reason field is empty, Then the system prevents submission and prompts for a reason.
Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, When a reason is provided, Then the system allows submission.
Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, When no polygon is uploaded, Then the system prevents submission and prompts for polygon upload.
Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, When a valid polygon is uploaded, Then the system allows submission.
Given a polygon is uploaded for monitoring, When the polygon defines a large area, Then the system accepts the polygon.
Given a polygon is uploaded for monitoring, When the polygon defines an overly small area, Then the system automatically filters out the polygon.
Given an image file has been previously uploaded, When a user attempts to upload the exact same image file again, Then the system prevents the duplicate upload.
Given a user is in two-period image comparison mode, Then the system disables access to the central image library.
Given a user is in two-period image comparison mode, Then tools for manual image annotation are available and functional.
Given the map viewer is active, Then historically uploaded imagery is displayed.
Given the map viewer displays imagery, When a user filters by a specific date or date range, Then only imagery matching the date criteria is displayed.
Given the map viewer displays imagery, When a user filters by an image-name keyword, Then only imagery whose names contain the keyword are displayed.
Given the map viewer displays imagery, When a user filters by image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone), Then only imagery of the selected type is displayed.
Given the map viewer is active, Then points of interest are displayed on the map.
Given a user opens a point of interest, Then a "locate" button is displayed.
Given a "locate" button is displayed for an opened point, When the user clicks the "locate" button, Then the map automatically zooms to the precise location of that point.
Given the map displays imagery, When the user zooms out beyond a predetermined scale, Then the displayed imagery automatically switches to a point-only representation.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, When the user attempts to pan the map, Then the map view remains locked.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode with panning locked, Then a "release drag mode" button is displayed.
Given the "release drag mode" button is displayed in comparison mode, When the user clicks the button, Then map panning functionality is restored.
Given imagery is displayed in the map viewer, Then a control is available to adjust image transparency.
Given the image transparency control is adjusted, Then the opacity of the displayed images changes visually.
Given the project view, Then projects are organized and displayed on a yearly basis.
Given an existing project, Then the user can create new suspected points within that project.
Given a user is creating a suspected point, When no polygon is uploaded, Then the system prevents saving and prompts for polygon upload.
Given a user is creating a suspected point, Then the system does not require or offer an option for image uploads.
Given suspected points are created for a specific year, Then all points pertaining to that year are grouped into a single visible layer.
Given the system functionality, Then a "New Survey Point Data" interface is accessible.

Given the "New Survey Point Data" interface is open, Then it includes input fields for 'survey serial number', 'drawing number', 'key monitoring zone', 'village', 'monitoring date', 'monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment)', 'volume (cubic meters)', 'area (square meters)', 'section', 'lot number', 'X/Y coordinates', 'longitude/latitude', and 'approximate location'.
Given the migrated MIS system, When a user performs any action that was available in the legacy MIS system, Then the action's core functionality executes as it did in the legacy system.
Given the migrated MIS system, Then its user interface has a redesigned appearance compared to the legacy system.
Given a list of suspected points, When a user selects one or more points, Then an option is available to integrate these points into the follow-up module.
Given the follow-up module is active, Then a schedule-tracking interface is displayed.
Given a follow-up item with a defined schedule, Then the schedule-tracking interface displays information about the remaining time.
Given a follow-up action is completed, When it is linked to survey-report management, Then the survey report includes the 'time' of completion.
Given a follow-up action is completed, When it is linked to survey-report management, Then the survey report includes a 'description' of the follow-up.
Given a follow-up action is completed, When it is linked to survey-report management, Then the survey report includes the 'action taken'.
Given a follow-up action is completed, When it is linked to survey-report management, Then the survey report includes any 'uploaded images' related to the follow-up.
test_scenarios: Initiate AI interpretation from Vendor A batch imagery and verify task creation and successful completion notification.
Initiate AI interpretation from user-uploaded individual images and verify task creation and successful completion notification.
After AI interpretation, confirm all suspected points and verify they are available for monitoring.
After AI interpretation, decline some suspected points and verify they are not moved to monitoring.
Place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring, providing a reason and a large-area polygon, and verify successful submission.
Attempt to place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring without providing a reason, verify system prevents submission and prompts for input.
Attempt to place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring without uploading a polygon, verify system prevents submission and prompts for polygon upload.
Attempt to upload an image file that has already been successfully uploaded, verify duplicate prevention mechanism triggers.
Enter two-period image comparison mode and verify the image library is inaccessible and manual annotation tools are available and functional.
Load the map viewer and verify historical imagery and various points are displayed correctly.
Apply various filters (date, image-name keyword, image type) to imagery in the map viewer and verify correct filtering results.
Open a point on the map, click the "locate" button, and verify the map automatically zooms to the precise location of the point.
Zoom the map viewer out past the predetermined scale and verify imagery automatically switches to a point-only display.
Enter comparison mode in the map viewer and verify panning is disabled.
In comparison mode, click the "release drag mode" button and verify map panning functionality is restored.
Adjust image transparency using the provided control and verify the visual change in opacity.
Create a new project and verify it is organized and displayed by year.
Create suspected points within an existing project, ensuring polygon upload is required and image upload is not permitted.
Verify all suspected points for a given year are managed within a single visible layer.
Access the "New Survey Point Data" interface and verify all specified fields are present and accept appropriate data types (e.g., numerical for volume/area, date format for monitoring date).
Perform several core functions (e.g., file upload, search, view) of the legacy MIS file management system in the new migrated system and verify identical functional behavior.
Visually inspect the migrated MIS system interface to confirm it aligns with the redesigned appearance.
Select multiple suspected points and verify they can be successfully integrated into the follow-up module.
Create a new follow-up item and verify the schedule-tracking interface displays information such as 'days remaining'.
Complete a follow-up action and verify that a new survey report is generated/updated with the correct time, description, action taken, and associated images.
edge_cases: Upload images with minimal visual or metadata differences to test the robustness of the duplicate prevention mechanism (e.g., 1-pixel change, EXIF data alteration).
Upload a polygon for monitoring that has an area precisely at the boundary of the 'overly small' filtering threshold.
Attempt to upload polygons with invalid geometry (e.g., self-intersecting, open, non-geospatial data) or unsupported file formats for monitoring/suspected points.
Initiate AI interpretation with an empty or corrupted image file and verify appropriate error handling and notification.
Filter map viewer imagery using a keyword or date range that yields no results.
Zoom the map viewer rapidly in and out, especially around the
unresolved_behaviors: Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., content, delivery method, target users).
Whether the suspected point layers should be split by county/city.
Interface design details for the MIS migration.
Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule.

decision_history: AI interpretation runs will generate background tasks in the task list, with notifications sent upon completion (D001).
AI interpretation will utilize two image sources: a one-time nationwide batch from Vendor A and individual user-uploaded images (D003).
After AI interpretation, users will confirm suspected points and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring (D004, D005).

Placing a point under monitoring requires providing a reason and uploading a corresponding polygon (D006, D007). Large-area polygons are valid, but overly small ones will be automatically filtered out (D008, D009).
A duplicate-prevention mechanism is required for image uploads due to large file sizes (D010, D011).
The two-period image comparison feature will no longer use the central image library; users will manually annotate images instead (D012, D013).
A dedicated on-site workstation will be installed to handle image comparison tasks, with the comparison model triggered by the client's Access Point (AP) (D014, D015).
The foundational map data will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D016).

The map viewer must be capable of loading and displaying historically uploaded imagery, supporting filtering by date, image-name keyword (type: satellite/aerial photo/drone), and displaying points on the map (D017, D018, D019, D020, D021).
Upon opening a point on the map, a 'locate' button will be displayed, and clicking it will automatically zoom the map to that point's location (D022, D023).
When the map is zoomed out beyond a predetermined scale, displayed imagery will automatically revert to a point-only representation (D024).
In comparison mode, the map view will be locked, preventing panning, with a 'release drag mode' button provided to restore panning functionality (D025, D026).
Users will have the ability to adjust image transparency in the map viewer, which is considered an important feature for orthophoto analysis (D027, D028).
The design of the new map viewer should reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D029).

Projects within the system will be organized by year, and suspected points can be created within these projects (D030, D031). Each year's suspected points will collectively form a single layer (D035).
Suspected points require an uploaded polygon, but no image upload is needed (D033, D034).

A new 'New Survey Point Data' interface must be incorporated, including fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone', referencing manual figure 2.13 for design guidance, and specifying additional fields such as survey serial number, monitoring method, and coordinates (D036, D037, D038, D039).

The existing MS file management system will be migrated to the new system due to information-security compliance requirements. Its user interface will be redesigned, but core functionality will remain unchanged (D040, D041, D042, D043).
The legacy system already has an email-based follow-up/reminder feature (D045).

The new follow-up module will allow selected suspected points to be integrated, require a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining), and has a planned dispatch interface (Shao-Chien has an initial draft) (D046, D047, D048).
After a follow-up action is completed, it will connect to survey-report management, requiring inclusion of details such as time, description, action taken, and any uploaded images (D049, D050).
The Illegal Excavation AI project is currently in the bidding process (D054).
The next project meeting, scheduled for 2026-05-15, will focus on discussing system architecture (D061).
The first requirements interview for the project is confirmed for 2026-05-20 (D063).
dependencies: Base map provision by GIS Provider B via GeoServer is a dependency for the map viewer functionality (D016).
The design of the new map viewer is dependent on referencing the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D029).
The implementation of the 'New Survey Point Data' interface is dependent on referencing manual figure 2.13 (D038).

A-Bao requires a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes (image source 	 interpretation task execution (workstation) 	 result output 	 monitoring database) for their review and subsequent work (D051, D052, D053).
Preparation for the first requirements interview requires consolidating existing interfaces, along with preparing the system architecture diagram and the data flow diagram (D064).
risks: The project's anticipated kick-off in mid-May is dependent on the bidding process proceeding smoothly (D055). Risk: Delays or complications in the bidding process could postpone the project start date, impacting overall timelines. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High)

An immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may be needed on the afternoon of the bid award day (D056). Risk: This tight turnaround could lead to rushed decisions, incomplete requirements gathering, or insufficient preparation if not anticipated and managed proactively. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium)
open_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., recipients, content, frequency)? (D002, D065)
Should the suspected-point layers be split and organized by county/city? (D032, D066)
What are the specific interface design details for the MIS migration? (D044, D067)
Who will receive follow-up notifications and according to what schedule for the new follow-up feature? (D068)
milestones: Project expected to kick off in mid-May (contingent on smooth bidding process).
Next project meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15 (Topic: System architecture).
Sherry to finish 'add suspected point' data interface (referencing figure 2.13) by 2026-05-18.
Sherry to prepare data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review by 2026-05-18.
Sherry to organize existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) for use in the requirements interview by 2026-05-20.
Immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation needed on the afternoon of the bid award day.

system_requirements: The system SHALL generate a background task for each AI interpretation run, similar to the CC platform's task operation.
The system SHALL send a notification upon the completion of each AI interpretation task.
The AI interpretation process SHALL support two distinct image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded directly by the user.
The system SHALL generate a list of suspected points upon completion of AI interpretation.
The system SHALL provide users with the option to confirm or reject suspected points.
The system SHALL allow users to place confirmed suspected points under a watch or monitoring status.
The system SHALL require a reason to be provided when a user places a suspected point under monitoring.
The system SHALL require a corresponding polygon to be uploaded when a user places a suspected point under monitoring.
The system SHALL accept polygons with large areas as valid for monitoring points.
The system SHALL automatically filter out polygons representing overly small areas when uploaded for monitoring.
The system SHALL implement a mechanism to prevent duplicate image uploads.
The two-period image comparison feature SHALL NOT retrieve images from the central image library.
The system SHALL require users to manually annotate images for two-period comparison.
The map viewer SHALL be capable of loading and displaying historically uploaded imagery.
The map viewer SHALL support filtering loaded imagery by date (upload or capture date).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering loaded imagery by image-name keyword.
The map viewer SHALL support filtering loaded imagery by type (e.g., satellite, aerial photo, drone footage).
The map viewer SHALL display points of interest on the map interface.
The map viewer SHALL display a 'locate' button when a point is opened.
The map viewer SHALL automatically zoom to the precise location of a point when its 'locate' button is clicked.
The map viewer SHALL automatically switch from imagery display to point-only representation when zoomed out beyond a predetermined scale.
The map viewer SHALL lock the map view, preventing panning, when in comparison mode.
The map viewer SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button in comparison mode to restore panning functionality.
The map viewer SHALL provide functionality for users to adjust the transparency level of displayed images.
The system SHALL organize projects on a yearly basis.
The system SHALL allow for the creation of suspected points within an existing project.
The system SHALL require an uploaded polygon for each suspected point.
The system SHALL NOT require image uploads for suspected points.
The system SHALL manage suspected points such that all points pertaining to a given year collectively form a single layer.
The system SHALL incorporate a 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone'.

The 'New Survey Point Data' form SHALL include the following fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.
The existing MS file management system SHALL be migrated and integrated into the new system.
The user interface for the migrated MIS system SHALL be redesigned.
The core functionality of the migrated MIS system SHALL be preserved and remain unchanged.
The follow-up module SHALL integrate items selected from suspected points.
The system SHALL provide a schedule-tracking interface within the follow-up feature, displaying information such as days remaining.
The system SHALL include a follow-up dispatch interface.
The system SHALL link completed follow-up actions to the survey-report management system.
The survey-report management system SHALL include the following details for linked follow-up actions: time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
data_flows: Images for AI interpretation originate from either Vendor A (batch) or user uploads.
AI interpretation tasks generate results that are used to create lists of suspected points for user confirmation.
Images for comparison flow from the client's Access Point (AP) to the on-site workstation.
The client's Access Point (AP) triggers the image comparison model on the on-site workstation.
Base map data flows from GIS Provider B, exposed via GeoServer, to the map viewer component.
AI Interpretation Data Flow (as requested by A-Bao): Image Source -> Interpretation Task Execution (on-site workstation) -> Result Output -> Monitoring Database.
Follow-up actions link to survey-report management, transferring details such as time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
Notifications for AI interpretation task completion are sent to users (inferred — needs confirmation).
User-uploaded polygons for monitoring points are stored in the system (inferred — needs confirmation).
Suspected points and their associated polygons are created within projects and organized into yearly layers (inferred — needs confirmation).
interface_contracts: Interface: AI Interpretation Task Completion Notification. Expected Payload: (details pending confirmation).
Interface: User Image Upload for AI Interpretation. Protocol: (inferred - needs confirmation, e.g., HTTP POST). Expected Payload: Image file (large size, duplicate prevention required).
Interface: Suspected Point Confirmation. Expected Payload: Point ID (string), User Confirmation (boolean), (inferred - needs confirmation: user ID, timestamp).

Interface: Suspected Point Monitoring. Expected Payload: Point ID (string), Monitoring Reason (string, required), Monitoring Polygon (geometry, required), (inferred - needs confirmation: user ID, timestamp).

Interface: 'New Survey Point Data' Creation. Protocol: (inferred - needs confirmation). Expected Payload: survey serial number (string), drawing number (string), key monitoring zone (string), village (string), monitoring date (date), monitoring method (enum: precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (float, cubic meters), area (float, square meters), section (string), lot number (string), X/Y coordinates (float pair), longitude/latitude (float pair), approximate location (string).
Interface: Follow-up Dispatch. Expected Payload: (details pending, referencing Shao-Chien's initial draft).
Interface: Survey Report Management Integration. Expected Payload: Follow-up Time (timestamp), Follow-up Description (string), Action Taken (string), Uploaded Images (list of image references/IDs).

constraints.text: Images SHALL NOT be uploaded more than once due to large file sizes.
A dedicated workstation SHALL be installed on-site for image comparison tasks.
The image comparison model SHALL be triggered by the client's Access Point (AP) which makes a call to the on-site workstation.
The base map data SHALL be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
The design of the new map viewer SHALL reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The design and implementation of the 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL reference manual figure 2.13.
The legacy MIS system migration is driven by information-security compliance requirements.

architectural_decisions.text: The project will implement AI interpretation functionality utilizing background tasks and supporting multiple image sources (Vendor A batch and user uploads).
The two-period image comparison feature will not use the central image library; instead, it will rely on manual user annotation.

The system will include a map viewer component with capabilities for displaying and filtering historical imagery, point display, location zooming, comparison mode locking, and image transparency adjustment.
Projects and suspected points will be managed with a yearly organizational structure, where suspected points are created within projects and grouped into yearly layers.
A 'New Survey Point Data' interface will be added to the system, including specified fields for data capture.
The existing MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, with its user interface redesigned but core functionality preserved.

The system will incorporate a new follow-up and reminder feature, including selection of suspected points, a schedule-tracking interface, a dispatch interface, and integration with survey-report management.

open_analysis_questions: Clarify the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., content, format, delivery method).
Clarify whether suspected point layers should be geographically subdivided by county/city for data organization and display.
Clarify the specific interface design details for the migrated MIS system.

stakeholder.business_summary: The Illegal Excavation AI project is currently in the bidding phase, with an anticipated kick-off in mid-May. This project will introduce AI-powered image analysis to detect potential illegal activities, along with a new map viewer and an improved system for tracking follow-up actions. We are also migrating a legacy file management system to ensure information security compliance.
key_impact: Significantly improves efficiency and accuracy in identifying potential illegal excavations using AI.
Provides users with an enhanced map viewer for better visualization, filtering, and management of historical imagery and suspected points.
Strengthens overall data security and compliance by updating an older file management system.
Streamlines the process for managing follow-up tasks and reporting on identified issues.
Ensures data integrity by preventing duplicate uploads of large image files.
decisions_needing_alignment: The specific details of the notification system for AI interpretation task completion need to be confirmed.
A decision is needed on whether the suspected points displayed on the map should be organized by county/city or kept as a single layer.
The exact design details for the user interface of the migrated legacy file management system require confirmation.
We need to clarify who will receive follow-up notifications and what the schedule for these reminders will be.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the precise details regarding how users will receive notifications once the AI has finished interpreting imagery?
Should the suspected points displayed on the map be divided and organized by county or city, or should they all be in one layer?
What is the final design for the user interface of the legacy system being migrated for security compliance?
Who should receive notifications for follow-up actions and what will be the schedule for these reminders?

issues.description: The acceptance criterion 'Given an AI interpretation task is running, When the task fails, Then a failure notification is sent to the user' infers behavior (failure notification) that is not explicitly stated in the source decision D001, which only mentions a notification upon 'completion'.
issues.suggestion: Revise this acceptance criterion to accurately reflect D001 or explicitly flag it as an inferred requirement needing confirmation.

issues.description: The edge case 'Zoom the map viewer rapidly in and out, especially around the ' is an incomplete sentence and too vague to be actionable.
issues.suggestion: Complete the sentence to provide a clear and actionable edge case, or remove it if the thought cannot be finalized.

review.reviewer_notes: The report generally maintains a high quality, with outputs largely traceable to the extracted decisions. Open questions are consistently identified across relevant roles. However, two minor quality issues were identified in the QA view: one acceptance criterion infers behavior not explicitly stated in the source, and one edge case is an incomplete and vague sentence.
